question: How quickly must the primary on-call engineer respond to a pager alert?
expected_answer_notes: Answer should mention acknowledgment within 10 minutes and triage within 15 minutes.

retrieved_docs.title: Engineering On-Call Expectations
retrieved_docs.text: Primary on-call engineers must acknowledge pager alerts within 10 minutes and begin triage within 15 minutes. If no acknowledgment occurs, the alert escalates automatically to the secondary on-call engineer and then to the engineering manager.

retrieved_docs.title: Hiring Interview Rubric
retrieved_docs.text: Interviewers score candidates across problem solving, communication, technical depth, and role alignment on a scale from 1 to 4. Written feedback should be submitted within 24 hours and must include at least two evidence-based observations.

retrieved_docs.title: Office Access Procedures
retrieved_docs.text: Employees may access the office from 07:00 to 22:00 using their badge. Guests must be registered by a host before arrival and remain escorted at all times outside reception and meeting rooms.

final_prompt:

If no acknowledgment occurs, the alert escalates automatically to the secondary on-call engineer and then to the engineering manager.
- [doc-011] Hiring Interview Rubric (recruiting): Interviewers score candidates across problem solving, communication, technical depth, and role alignment on a scale from 1 to 4. Written feedback should be submitted within 24 hours and must include at least two evidence-based observations.
- [doc-010] Office Access Procedures (facilities): Employees may access the office from 07:00 to 22:00 using their badge. Guests must be registered by a host before arrival and remain escorted at all times outside reception and meeting rooms.
Question: How quickly must the primary on-call engineer respond to a pager alert?
Answer:
answer: The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge pager alerts within **10 minutes**.
